User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account in the Viewora AI Coach application, storing their profile information, token balance, and gamification level.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification and communication.

auro_balance.description: The current number of tokens available to the user for AI analysis.

level_name.description: The user's current gamification level (e.g., Basic, Mid, Pro).

current_xp.description: Experience points earned by the user, contributing to their gamification level.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp of the user's last login.

profileIndex.description: The user's calculated photographic profile index. This is calculated on-demand.

UserProfileIndex.title: User Profile Index
UserProfileIndex.description: Stores a calculated index of a user's photographic profile based on their best work.

Photo.title: Photo
Photo.description: Represents a photo submitted by a user for AI analysis, including the image URL and the AI's feedback.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Photo entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who submitted this photo. (Relationship: User 1:N Photo)

imageUrl.description: The URL of the photo image.

filePath.description: The path to the photo file in Firebase Storage.

aiFeedback.description: The raw JSON data from the AI analysis.

adaptiveFeedback.description: The human-readable adaptive feedback generated based on the user's level and the photo's analysis data.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the photo was submitted and analyzed.

AcademyLesson.title: AcademyLesson
AcademyLesson.description: Represents a single micro-learning lesson within the Viewora Academy, providing educational content to users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AcademyLesson entity.

level.description: The difficulty level of the lesson. One of: 'Temel', 'Orta', 'İleri'.

category.description: The specific topic/category of the lesson from the curriculum (e.g., 'Pozlama Temelleri', 'Görsel Hikâye Anlatımı').

learningObjective.description: The skill the user will gain from this lesson.

theory.description: The main educational content/theory for the lesson.

analysisCriteria.description: A list of 3 technical criteria for a photo to be considered 'successful' according to this lesson.

practiceTask.description: A specific, actionable shooting task for the user.

auroNote.description: An explanation of why this lesson's analysis provides a professional perspective.

imageUrl.description: The URL of an example photograph associated with the lesson.

imageHint.description: Keywords used to find a suitable image for the lesson.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the lesson was generated.

UserLessonProgress.title: UserLessonProgress
UserLessonProgress.description: Tracks a user's progress through the Viewora Academy lessons, recording which lessons they have completed.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserLessonProgress entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User making progress. (Relationship: User 1:N UserLessonProgress)

lessonId.description: Reference to the AcademyLesson being tracked. (Relationship: AcademyLesson 1:N UserLessonProgress)

isCompleted.description: Indicates whether the user has marked this lesson as completed.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the lesson was marked as completed by the user.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records financial and token-related transactions, such as token purchases, gifts, or token spending for AI analysis.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User involved in the transaction. (Relationship: User 1:N Transaction)

amount.description: The number of tokens involved in this transaction (positive for gain, negative for spend).

currencyAmount.description: The monetary amount of the transaction, if applicable (e.g., for purchases).

currency.description: The currency used for the transaction, if applicable (e.g., 'TL').

paymentProviderTransactionId.description: External transaction ID from the payment provider (e.g., Iyzico/Stripe), if applicable.

Group.title: Group
Group.description: Represents a user-created community, class, or workshop within the Viewora application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Group entity.

name.description: The display name of the group.

description.description: A short description of the group's purpose or theme.

ownerId.description: The UID of the user who created and owns the group.

memberIds.description: A list of user UIDs who are members of this group.

maxMembers.description: The maximum number of members this group can have, based on the owner's level.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the group was created.

GroupInvite.title: GroupInvite
GroupInvite.description: Represents a pending invitation for a user to join a group. This is a central collection.

groupName.description: Denormalized for easy display in notifications.

toUserId.description: The UID of the user who is invited.

GroupAssignment.title: Group Assignment
GroupAssignment.description: An assignment or task given by a group owner to the members.

AssignmentSubmission.title: Assignment Submission
AssignmentSubmission.description: A member's submission for a group assignment.

GroupEvent.title: Group Event
GroupEvent.description: An event or trip planned for a group.

rsvps.description: A map of user IDs to their RSVP status ('attending', 'declined').

GroupCompetition.title: Group Competition
GroupCompetition.description: A competition held within a group.

CompetitionEntry.title: Competition Entry
CompetitionEntry.description: A member's entry for a group competition.

GroupTraining.title: Group Training
GroupTraining.description: A training or educational material assigned to a group.

linkedLessonId.description: Optional ID of a lesson from the main Academy.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles including authentication details, token balance, and gamification level. This collection is private, with documents owned by the user matching the '{userId}' wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

definition.description: A subcollection storing photos submitted by a specific user for AI analysis. Documents are private and owned by the user matching the '{userId}' wildcard. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence and easier querying.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns these photos.

params.name: photoId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific photo.

definition.description: A subcollection tracking a specific user's progress through the academy lessons. Documents are private and owned by the user matching the '{userId}' wildcard. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'lessonId' for authorization independence and easier querying.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user whose lesson progress is being tracked.

params.name: progressId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user's lesson progress entry.

definition.description: A subcollection recording all financial and token-related transactions for a specific user. Documents are private and owned by the user matching the '{userId}' wildcard. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence and easier querying.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user involved in the transaction.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific transaction.

definition.description: Stores group information, including owner and member lists. Access is restricted to members.

params.name: groupId
params.description: The unique identifier for the group.

definition.description: A subcollection for photos shared within a specific group. Access is limited to group members.

params.name: groupId
params.description: The unique identifier of the group that owns these photos.

params.name: photoId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific photo within the group.

definition.description: A global collection of all pending and processed group invitations.

params.name: inviteId
params.description: The unique ID for the group invitation.

definition.description: A top-level collection containing all micro-learning lessons for the Viewora Academy. These documents are public and read-only for all authenticated users.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier for an academy lesson.

definition.description: Stores weekly snapshots of user profile scores for tracking progress.

params.name: snapshotId
params.description: The unique identifier for a weekly snapshot.

definition.description: Stores assignments for a specific group. Writable only by group owner.
Stores member submissions for a specific assignment. Writable only by the submitting member.
Stores events (trips, meetings) for a specific group. Writable by owner, but members can update their own RSVP status.
Stores competitions for a specific group. Writable only by group owner.
Stores member entries for a specific competition. Writable only by the submitting member, members can vote.
Stores trainings for a specific group. Writable only by group owner.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for Viewora AI Coach is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and predictable access patterns to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. The core strategy revolves around path-based authorization and denormalization.

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved primarily by structuring all user-specific data as subcollections under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection. For instance, user photos are stored at `/users/{userId}/photos/{photoId}`, user lesson progress at `/users/{userId}/lessonProgress/{progressId}`, and transactions at `/users/{userId}/transactions/{transactionId}`. This design ensures that security rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, eliminating the need for expensive and potentially insecure `get()` calls to determine ownership.

**Group Structure:** The new `/groups/{groupId}` collection introduces a community dimension. Access to a group's data is primarily controlled by a `memberIds` array within each group document. This avoids complex joins and allows security rules to efficiently check for membership (`request.auth.uid in resource.data.memberIds`). Group-specific content, like the shared photo gallery at `/groups/{groupId}/photos/{photoId}`, inherits its permissions from the parent group, ensuring only members can view or contribute. New subcollections for assignments, competitions, events, and trainings under each group follow this pattern, with write access restricted to group owners and read access granted to members, ensuring a secure and organized environment for group activities.

**QAPs (Queries as Authorization Primitives):** The structural segregation directly supports secure `list` operations. Because all documents in a path like `/users/{userId}/photos` inherently belong to the `userId` specified in the path, any `list` query on this path will only return documents that the authenticated user is authorized to access. This same principle applies to groups, where queries on a group's subcollections are protected by membership checks. Similarly, the `academyLessons` collection's top-level, public read-only nature simplifies security for `list` operations, making them accessible to all authenticated users.
